particulars: little beach shacks F8216106008 Thanks | Transfer Debit
sl_question: Need invoice for proper GST treatment or kindly confirm the nature of this transaction

current_verdict.nature: $4,000 to 'little beach shacks' — accommodation booking on a business trip.
current_verdict.your_comment: Nature: $4,000 accommodation payment to 'little beach shacks' — but the particulars read 'Transfer Debit' with reference F8216106008 and 'Thanks', i.e. a direct bank transfer to the host, not a card/merchant booking. Treat as a business-trip accommodation cost, project ACT-IN, account Travel & accommodation. GST: a P2P transfer to a small/private accommodation host very likely has NO tax invoice (many such hosts are not GST-registered), so default to GST Free Expenses pending the host's tax invoice — do NOT claim GST on Expenses on the strength of a booking confirmation that may not be a valid tax invoice. SL asked us to confirm the nature: please confirm (a) this was an ACT business trip (not private), and (b) whether the host issued a tax invoice showing GST. If a valid tax invoice is provided, switch to GST on Expenses; if private/non-registered host, it stays GST Free; if the stay was personal it is Drawings (880). No receipt on file — receipt_status is a genuine GAP, not a confirmed business booking.

particulars: INTERNET TRANSFER Thanks | Transfer Debit
sl_question: Need invoice for proper GST treatment or kindly confirm the nature of this transaction

current_verdict.nature: $1,439.60 'INTERNET TRANSFER' — likely the AAMI Commercial Motor insurance direct debit (a forwarded AAMI Commercial Motor New Business Direct Debit email exists in hi@).
current_verdict.your_comment: This line is a bare 'INTERNET TRANSFER Thanks | Transfer Debit' for $1,439.60 — the wording 'INTERNET TRANSFER ... Thanks' reads as a person-to-person bank transfer (a 'thanks' payment to an individual), not a direct debit to AAMI. The 'AAMI Commercial Motor Insurance' email is an amount/inference match only: it shares NO word with the particulars, so per the receipt-trap rule it is a RECEIPT_VENDOR_MISMATCH, not a real document for this line. A genuine AAMI debit would carry an AAMI/Suncorp/DIRECT DEBIT descriptor, not 'INTERNET TRANSFER Thanks'. I'm therefore NOT treating this as insurance and NOT applying GST on Expenses. Confirming the nature: who was this $1,439.60 transfer paid to and what for? If it's a P2P transfer to an individual there's no tax invoice (GST Free, confirm nature); if it genuinely turns out to be the AAMI premium, note that the stamp-duty portion is GST-free so GST on Expenses can't apply to the full amount. Please confirm the payee/purpose before I code it — do not assume AAMI.

particulars: HPJCFBWSB5 Humanitix Ltd A CURIOUS TRACTO | Interbank Credit
sl_question: Need invoice for proper GST treatment or kindly confirm the nature of this transaction

current_verdict.nature: Humanitix remittance of $1,150 — event-ticketing platform paying through ticket sales to ACT. This is event/ticket income (fee-for-service), not a grant.
current_verdict.your_comment: Humanitix $1,150 Interbank Credit = event-ticketing platform remitting ticket-sales income to ACT — this is event/ticket revenue (fee-for-service), NOT a grant. Code as Other Revenue (event income), project ACT-IN as the general bucket pending event confirmation. The Dext "Humanitix - 2025-11-05 - HPJCFBWSB5.pdf" receipt is a GENUINE match (ref code HPJCFBWSB5 ties to the line particulars), so receipt is supported — but it is a platform remittance statement, treat as nature-confirmation evidence, not a clean per-ticket tax invoice. TWO things to confirm before locking GST: (1) Net-vs-gross — Humanitix usually remits ticket sales NET of its booking/platform fee, so $1,150 is likely the net figure. Income GST should be 1/11th of the GROSS ticket sales, with the Humanitix fee booked separately (its own GST), NOT simply 1/11th of $1,150. Pull the Humanitix settlement statement to split gross sales vs fee before posting. (2) GST on Income only applies if the sole trader was GST-registered and the event was a taxable supply at the time — confirm registration status for the period. If both confirmed, GST on Income is correct; otherwise GST-free. Grant-vs-revenue call and GST direction (on income) are right; the open item is the gross/net treatment and the registration check.

particulars: MALL MEDICAL CENTRE Alice Springs | Credit Card Purchase
sl_question: Need invoice for proper GST treatment or kindly confirm the nature of this transaction

current_verdict.nature: Mall Medical Centre, Alice Springs (Mparntwe) — medical/clinic charge on the ACT business card while on the ground in Alice Springs. Likely a travel-related medical or staff/contractor health expense tied to the Oonchiumpa work. Need to confirm whether it was a genuine business medical cost (e.g. travel-required check/treatment for someone working on the trip) or a personal medical visit.
current_verdict.account: Travel - National (or Drawings if personal medical)
current_verdict.your_comment: This is a charge from Mall Medical Centre in Alice Springs (Mparntwe) on the ACT business card on 21 May 2026 for $201.28. A doctor/clinic visit is a personal health service in nature, so my default is to code it to my Drawings (880 - Nicholas Marchesi), not a business travel expense — being on a business trip doesn't make a personal medical visit deductible. If it turns out this was a genuinely business-required medical cost (e.g. a mandated check tied to the work up there), let me know and we can revisit. Either way the GST treatment is GST-Free, not GST on Expenses: medical and health services are GST-free supplies in Australia (Subdiv 38-B), so there's no GST credit to claim even with an invoice. I haven't tied this to any specific project — please don't assume Oonchiumpa (ACT-OO) just from the Alice Springs location; if it's coded to a project at all it would be ACT-IN. No tax invoice on file; I can request a receipt from the clinic if you need it on record, but it won't change the GST-free treatment. Please confirm with me before finalising.

current_verdict.your_comment: This is a $200 reimbursement to Shane Bloomfield for a Bunnings purchase he made for BG Fit (ACT-BG) — particulars 'Bunnings thanks', and it's a bank Transfer Debit (P2P to an individual), so there is no tax invoice attached to the transfer itself. The receipt currently on file is a Woolworths receipt (Woolworths-receipt.pdf) — that is a wrong amount-only match (RECEIPT_VENDOR_MISMATCH), please ignore it; it does not substantiate this line. IMPORTANT on GST: with no valid tax invoice on file, no GST is claimable right now — code this GST Free / treat as a receipt GAP, NOT 'GST on Expenses'. Bunnings is a domestic supplier, so ONCE Shane provides the actual Bunnings tax invoice we can switch it to GST on Expenses and claim the 1/11th. Until that invoice is produced it stays GST Free. Project ACT-BG, account 430 (subject to project confirmation).

particulars: Audible Limited AU MELBOURNE | Credit Card Purchase
sl_question: Is this an office expense or Drawings - Nicholas Marchesi?

current_verdict.nature: Audible audiobook subscription
current_verdict.account: 485 Subscriptions (office expense) - or 880 Drawings if personal
current_verdict.your_comment: Audible Limited AU ($16.45, NAB Visa #8815). SL asked one thing — office expense or Drawings — so decide it, don't punt. An audiobook subscription is presumptively personal/lifestyle; unless Ben confirms specific work use, code 880 Drawings - Nicholas Marchesi with NO GST claimed. Only if Ben confirms it is genuinely for work does it become 485 Subscriptions with GST on Expenses (Audible AU is GST-registered, the $16.45 is GST-inclusive — but you cannot assert GST on Expenses while the item may be Drawings; the two are mutually exclusive and the proposal states both). On the receipt: the on-file receipt is 'Receipt from Amazon / Amazon-receipt.jpg' — no shared word with 'Audible', so RECEIPT_VENDOR_MISMATCH stands and we do not rely on it (correct call; Audible bills via Amazon so the monthly Audible email is the real record if business use is confirmed). Net: do not default to claiming GST — default to Drawings/no-GST and let Ben upgrade it to a business subscription. RECEIPT FOUND: Audible order receipt (donotreply@audible.com.au, 'your order is complete', $16.45 confirmed) in benjamin@act.place — so a receipt does exist. The remaining call is still office-expense vs Drawings (an audiobook sub is presumptively personal).
